column1: What does a VLA (vision-language-action) model output, and how?
column2: Robot actions — usually as discrete action tokens decoded from a VLM backbone, or as continuous actions via a diffusion/flow head.

column1: What is action tokenization in a VLA?
column2: Discretizing continuous robot actions into tokens an autoregressive model can predict (e.g. the FAST tokenizer).

column1: What are the System 1 / System 2 parts of pi-0.5-style VLAs?
column2: System 2 = slow semantic reasoning emitting discrete tokens; System 1 = fast flow-matching head turning them into continuous control.

column1: Why is SmolVLA notable, and why start with it?
column2: ~450M params, open, runs on consumer/Colab hardware, trained on community LeRobot data; lowest-friction VLA to actually run.

column1: Role of the VLM backbone in a VLA?
column2: Encodes image + language instruction into features that condition the action decoder.
Discrete = autoregressive token prediction (simple, fast). Diffusion/flow = sample continuous actions (smoother, better for high-DoF).
NVIDIA open humanoid VLA foundation model, improved over N1, trained with large synthetic data (GR00T-Dreams).
HuggingFace open framework with a unified policy + dataset API for VLAs (GR00T, pi0/pi0.5, SmolVLA).

column1: Why fine-tune a pretrained VLA instead of training from scratch?
column2: Pretrained policies carry broad manipulation priors, so a little task data adapts them to your robot/task.
Store demo trajectories as embeddings; at inference retrieve similar demos to condition or warm-start the policy.
Parameter-efficient fine-tuning: adapt a small set of params (LoRA, adapters, prompts) instead of all weights.

column1: Common sim benchmarks for evaluating VLAs?
column2: LIBERO and Meta-World in sim; real tasks on SO-100/SO-101 arms.
Gradually add Gaussian noise over T steps until the image is approximately pure noise.
Learn to denoise step by step, typically by predicting the noise added at each timestep.

column1: What does the UNet predict in a diffusion model?
column2: The noise (or equivalently the score) to remove at a given timestep.

column1: Role of the VAE in latent diffusion (Stable Diffusion)?
column2: Compress the image into a latent space, run diffusion there (cheaper), then decode back to pixels.
DDIM is deterministic and needs far fewer steps, so generation is much faster.
Blend conditional and unconditional predictions to strengthen prompt adherence, controlled by a guidance scale.
How much noise is added to the init image; higher strength means more deviation from the original.
Adds spatial conditioning (depth, edges, pose) to a frozen diffusion model via trainable zero-convolution branches.
The score is the gradient of log-density; denoising follows the score toward higher-probability (more realistic) data.

column1: Why use diffusion for robot policies?
column2: It models multimodal action distributions well and samples smooth high-DoF action sequences.
Simultaneously estimate the camera/robot trajectory and build a map of an unknown environment.
ORB feature tracking, local mapping, loop closure, and pose-graph / bundle-adjustment optimization.
Recognizing a previously visited place to correct accumulated trajectory drift.
Jointly optimize camera poses and 3D points to minimize total reprojection error.
SLAM that uses 3D Gaussian Splatting as the map representation for dense, photorealistic reconstruction.

column1: Why is SLAM only awareness-level for an Applied-GenAI/VLA target?
column2: These roles rarely build SLAM; you should know what it is and when it is needed, not implement it.
Lower precision means smaller and faster but higher accuracy risk; calibrate or fine-tune to recover quality.
Post-training quantization needs no retraining; quantization-aware training simulates quantization during training for better accuracy.
Freeze the base weights and train small low-rank A times B adapters on selected layers — cheap fine-tuning.
LoRA applied on top of a 4-bit quantized base model, letting you fine-tune large models on small GPUs.
Both are 4-bit post-training quant for LLMs; GPTQ uses second-order error, AWQ protects salient weights by activation importance.
NVIDIA 4-bit float format (block size 16, two-level scaling) for Blackwell GPUs; about 2-3x throughput vs FP8.
IO-aware exact attention that tiles work to avoid materializing the N by N matrix; FA3 adds asynchrony and FP8.
Train a small student model to match a large teacher's soft output distribution.
Compiles a model into an optimized inference engine (kernel fusion, precision calibration) for NVIDIA GPUs.
A small draft model proposes tokens that the large model verifies in parallel — faster generation, identical outputs.

column1: Why compress a VLA for robots?
column2: Real-time control needs low latency on limited on-robot compute, so you must hit a tight latency/memory budget.
Contrastive: pull matching image-text pairs together and push non-matching apart in a shared embedding space.
Meta's segment-anything-with-concepts model: detect, segment, and track objects from short noun-phrase prompts.
NMS-free end-to-end detection (also DFL-free), faster especially on edge.
Predict per-pixel depth from a single RGB image (e.g. Depth Anything 3, Apple Depth Pro).
An object's 3D position plus 3D orientation; FoundationPose estimates it for novel objects from a CAD model or reference images.
State-of-the-art self-supervised vision features (uses Gram anchoring); a strong frozen backbone for downstream tasks.
NeRF uses an MLP with volume rendering (slow); 3DGS uses explicit Gaussians rasterized in real time.

column1: What is a world model (DreamerV3, Genie)?
column2: A learned simulator that predicts future observations/states, used for planning or generating training data.
Policies trained in simulation fail in reality due to visual and physics mismatch.
Randomize textures, lighting, and physics in sim so the real world looks like just another variation.
Intersection area divided by union area of two boxes; thresholded to match detections.
Keep the highest-confidence box and drop overlapping duplicates above an IoU threshold.
A loss that down-weights easy examples to focus training on hard ones — handles class imbalance in detection.
Precision = TP/(TP+FP); recall = TP/(TP+FN).
Mean over classes of average precision (area under the precision-recall curve), often averaged over IoU thresholds.

column1: How does the Adam optimizer work?
column2: Adaptive per-parameter learning rates using running estimates of the gradient's first and second moments.
An approximate nearest-neighbor structure (HNSW, IVF) trading a little recall for large search speedups.
A hierarchical navigable small-world graph enabling fast high-recall approximate nearest-neighbor search.
Cosine compares direction/angle (good for normalized embeddings); L2 is Euclidean distance.

column1: Why use a vector DB in a VLA pipeline?
column2: To retrieve similar past observations or demos to condition or warm-start the policy.
